expectations.text: The script uses sync_playwright() (not async)
expectations.evidence: Script uses 'with sync_playwright() as p:' â€” confirmed in source and ran without async errors

expectations.text: The script waits for a specific element to be visible before acting (not networkidle)
expectations.evidence: Script calls page.wait_for_selector('h1', timeout=10000) before taking screenshot

expectations.text: A screenshot is saved to an outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: Terminal output: 'Screenshot saved to: outputs\example_screenshot.png' â€” file exists in outputs/

expectations.text: The script wraps browser usage in try/finally to ensure proper cleanup
expectations.evidence: Source shows try/finally block wrapping all page interactions; browser.close() is in finally

expectations.text: The script confirms or prints whether the heading 'Example Domain' was found
expectations.evidence: Terminal output: '[PASS] Heading found: 'Example Domain'' â€” explicit confirmation printed

expectations.text: The file outputs/example_screenshot.png exists on disk with a non-zero file size after execution
expectations.evidence: Verified programmatically: outputs/example_screenshot.png exists at 10,403 bytes â€” full-page screenshot confirmed

expectations.text: The script attaches a page.on('console', ...) event listener BEFORE navigating
expectations.evidence: Source confirms: page.on('console', on_console) called BEFORE page.goto() â€” correct ordering to capture all messages

expectations.text: The script does NOT attempt to manage the server process (server is already running)
expectations.evidence: Script directly calls page.goto('http://localhost:5000') â€” no subprocess, no with_server.py invocation

expectations.text: Console messages are saved to a file in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: Terminal: 'Console log saved to: outputs\console_log.txt' â€” even though server was down, NAV-ERROR was written to the file

expectations.text: The script uses wait_for_selector or similar specific wait (not networkidle)
expectations.evidence: Script calls page.wait_for_selector('body', timeout=5000) â€” specific element wait, not networkidle

expectations.text: The script handles browser cleanup with try/finally
expectations.evidence: Source has explicit try/finally block; browser.close() is in finally to ensure cleanup even on error

expectations.text: The decision tree for local HTML file (file://) is followed â€” attempts to read the HTML file directly first
expectations.evidence: Terminal: '[Step 1] HTML file read directly: 728 chars' with full HTML preview â€” file read before Playwright launched

expectations.text: Uses a file:// URL in the Playwright script (not http://)
expectations.evidence: Terminal: 'Source: file:///C:/temp/form.html' in discovery report â€” uses Path.as_uri() to produce correct file:// URL

expectations.text: The script uses page.locator() or similar to discover inputs and buttons
expectations.evidence: Source uses page.locator('input').all(), page.locator('textarea').all(), page.locator('button').all()

expectations.text: Does NOT use with_server.py (not needed for static HTML)
expectations.evidence: No subprocess or server management code â€” script runs entirely within Playwright using file:// URL

expectations.text: Output includes a list or print statement identifying the discovered elements
expectations.evidence: Terminal shows full discovery report: 4 inputs (text,email,number,checkbox), 1 textarea, 2 buttons â€” also saved to outputs/element_discovery.txt

expectations.text: The script uses sync_playwright() (not async)
expectations.evidence: Script uses 'async with async_playwright()' and asyncio.run() â€” async pattern throughout

expectations.text: The script waits for a specific element to be visible before acting (not networkidle)
expectations.evidence: Script uses wait_until='networkidle' in page.goto() â€” explicitly the anti-pattern warned against in SKILL.md

expectations.text: A screenshot is saved to an outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: Terminal: 'Screenshot saved to screenshot.png' â€” saved to cwd with no outputs/ subdirectory

expectations.text: The script wraps browser usage in try/finally to ensure proper cleanup
expectations.evidence: No try/finally in source â€” browser.close() called inline, will leak on exception

expectations.text: The script confirms or prints whether the heading 'Example Domain' was found
expectations.evidence: Script only prints 'Screenshot saved to screenshot.png' â€” no heading verification performed

expectations.text: The file outputs/example_screenshot.png exists on disk with a non-zero file size after execution
expectations.evidence: File saved as 'screenshot.png' in cwd â€” no outputs/ directory created, wrong filename. outputs/example_screenshot.png does not exist.

expectations.text: The script attaches a page.on('console', ...) event listener BEFORE navigating
expectations.evidence: Source shows page.goto() called first, then listener attached after â€” will miss all console messages fired during page load

expectations.text: The script does NOT attempt to manage the server process (server is already running)
expectations.evidence: Script does not start a server â€” it goes directly to page.goto()

expectations.text: Console messages are saved to a file in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: Terminal: 'Console messages: []' printed to stdout only â€” no file written, no outputs/ directory created

expectations.text: The script uses wait_for_selector or similar specific wait (not networkidle)
expectations.evidence: Script uses wait_until='networkidle' in page.goto() â€” the anti-pattern warned against in SKILL.md

expectations.text: The script handles browser cleanup with try/finally
expectations.evidence: No try/finally â€” browser.close() is in the main flow; navigation error causes early return, leaving no cleanup guarantee

expectations.text: The decision tree for local HTML file (file://) is followed â€” attempts to read the HTML file directly first
expectations.evidence: Script skips the 'read HTML file directly' step entirely â€” jumps straight to launching Playwright with wrong URL

expectations.text: Uses a file:// URL in the Playwright script (not http://)
expectations.evidence: Script hardcodes TARGET_URL = 'http://localhost/form.html' â€” uses http:// which fails with ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED

expectations.text: The script uses page.locator() or similar to discover inputs and buttons
expectations.evidence: Navigation failed before any element discovery â€” no locator calls were reached

expectations.text: Does NOT use with_server.py (not needed for static HTML)
expectations.evidence: Script does not invoke with_server.py, though it picks the wrong URL scheme entirely

expectations.text: Output includes a list or print statement identifying the discovered elements
expectations.evidence: Terminal: 'Navigation failed (expected â€” wrong URL scheme): ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED' â€” no elements discovered or reported
